Are You Looking to Build a Career That Contributes to a Brighter Future?

At Hempel, success isn’t just about meeting targets – it’s about collaborating, innovating, and making a real impact.

What Are We Looking For?

As a mid-senior BI Analyst, you will play a pivotal role in transforming financial data into actionable insights through advanced Power BI reporting. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams, drive data-driven decision-making, and continuously improve reporting processes within an Agile environment.

Key Responsibilities

• Data Collection & Processing: Gather, clean, and process large datasets from diverse financial sources to ensure robust and reliable analysis.
• Advanced Data Analysis: Analyze complex financial data to identify trends, patterns, and key business drivers, delivering actionable insights to stakeholders.
• Power BI Report Development: Design, develop, and maintain sophisticated Power BI dashboards and reports tailored to Finance and business needs.
• Stakeholder Engagement: Present findings and recommendations to Finance, Commercial, Operations, and Digital teams, ensuring clarity and impact.
• Continuous Improvement: Proactively identify and implement opportunities to enhance financial processes, reporting automation, and data quality.
• Agile Collaboration: Work within Agile teams, leveraging Microsoft DevOps for backlog management, sprint planning, and iterative delivery

Key Success Criteria’s / KPIs

• Expansion and maintenance of a self-service Power BI reports portfolio.
• High stakeholder satisfaction with reporting solutions and insights.
• Demonstrated impact on data-driven decision-making in Finance.
• Continuous improvement of reporting processes and data quality

Required Education, Experience And Job-related Skills

• Master’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field.
• 3–6 years of hands-on experience in designing, developing, and implementing BI solutions using Microsoft Power BI in a Finance context.
• Strong understanding of financial reporting, accounting, or controlling.
• Experience working in Agile teams; familiarity with Microsoft DevOps is a strong plus.
• Knowledge of DataBricks is an advantage.
• Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
• Proficiency in English

General behavioural competencies most relevant for the job

• User-focused and results-driven.
• Strong planning and organizational skills.
• Trusted collaborator with a proactive approach.
• Analytical mindset with attention to detail.

Application due

2026-05-31

Seniority Level

Associate

Job Functions

Analyst, Finance

Industry

Chemicals

At Hempel, you’re welcomed to a global community of +7000 colleagues around the world. A workplace built on shared trust, mutual respect and support for each other.

We’re on a mission to double our impact. To succeed, we need bright minds and team players. Which is why, from day one, your input, ideas and initiative are not only welcomed, but expected. In return, you will enjoy great opportunities for development in a growing global company – and be part of the solution by contributing to our global impact.

At Hempel, we are committed to everyone feeling safe, valued and treated equally, in an environment where each employee can bring their authentic selves to work.  We believe that inclusion is key to innovation and by bringing together the most diverse perspectives and talents, we can achieve great things, together.

Head of Accounting India

Location: Pune , India
Reporting To: CFO

Function: Finance & Accounting
Role Overview The Head of Accounting India will lead the full spectrum of accounting and financial reporting across all Indian legal entities. This role is accountable for ensuring best-in-class financial integrity, regulatory compliance, and robust internal controls, while aligning with global finance standards.

As a key member of the finance leadership team, the role will partner closely with business leaders, global stakeholders, and external advisors to drive operational excellence and strategic initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

• Lead end-to-end accounting operations including General Ledger, Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, Fixed Assets, and Intercompany accounting

• Ensure accurate and timely monthly, quarterly, and annual financial close in compliance with Ind AS, IFRS, and statutory requirements

• Own and manage statutory audits, tax audits, and internal audits, act as primary liaison with auditors and regulatory bodies

• Ensure full compliance with the Companies Act, GST, Income Tax, Transfer Pricing, and other applicable regulations

• Ensure compliance with applicable statute, listing regulations, and regulatory requirements, including those prescribed by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI)

• Establish and strengthen internal controls, including SOX compliance, and drive continuous process improvements

• Review and approve financial statements, disclosures, and management reporting packs

• Partner with FP&A, Tax, Treasury, and Business Finance teams to support business decisions

• Build, lead, and develop a high-performing accounting organization across multiple entities

• Support strategic initiatives including M&A, restructuring, and ERP/system implementations (SAP/Oracle)

Stakeholder Management

• CFO and Executive Leadership Team

• Global Finance and Accounting teams

• External auditors, tax advisors, and regulatory authorities

• Business and functional leadership teams

Qualifications & Experience

• Chartered Accountant (CA) mandatory

• 15+ years of progressive experience in accounting, financial reporting, and controllership

• Proven experience managing multiple legal entities in complex environments (MNC, GCC, or listed company)

• Strong expertise in Ind AS, IFRS, statutory audits, and Indian taxation

• Experience in a listed company environment is preferred

Skills & Competencies

• Strong leadership and team-building capabilities

• Deep Technical accounting expertise with sound judgment

• Excellent stakeholder management and influencing skills

• Strategic mindset with high attention to detail

• Experience with ERP systems such as SAP or Oracle

Why This Role
This is a high-impact leadership opportunity offering visibility at the executive level and the chance to shape the accounting function within a dynamic and evolving organization. The role provides strong exposure to global finance operations and a clear path for career advancement.

Position Overview

We are looking to engage an experienced Capital Markets Professional with 10-15 years of hands-on experience in executing IPO transactions in India, spanning either SME IPOs or Mainboard IPOs or both. The role is designed for professionals who have directly led or managed IPO execution at merchant banking firms, capital markets advisory firms, or boutique investment banks. Candidates must bring practical, end-to-end experience across the IPO lifecycle - from regulatory coordination and documentation through investor engagement and transaction closure. This position is ideal for ex-Merchant Bankers, IPO Advisors, ECM professionals, or capital markets specialists with a strong track record in Indian primary market transactions.

Key Responsibilities

IPO Transaction Execution

- Lead and manage end-to-end execution of SME IPO and Mainboard IPO mandates

- Coordinate with SEBI, Stock Exchanges (NSE/BSE), Merchant Bankers, Legal Advisors, Registrars, and Auditors throughout the transaction lifecycle

- Oversee the preparation, review, and filing of DRHP, RHP, Prospectus, and all offer documents

- Guide issuers on IPO structuring, valuation positioning, pricing strategy, and regulatory compliance

- Manage listing readiness assessments and ensure fulfilment of exchange compliance requirements

Transaction Management

- Drive the IPO timeline, due diligence process, and end-to-end documentation workflow

- Work closely with promoters, CFOs, and internal teams to ensure seamless execution and timely milestone delivery

- Coordinate with all transaction intermediaries including Merchant Bankers, Legal Counsels, Registrar & Transfer Agents, PR agencies, and Market Makers (for SME IPOs)

Capital Markets Advisory

- Advise companies on optimal capital raising strategies across instruments including SME IPO, Mainboard IPO, Rights Issues, Preferential Allotments, Qualified Institutional Placements (QIP), and Private Placements

- Evaluate IPO readiness of companies covering corporate governance, financial reporting standards, and compliance frameworks

- Provide strategic guidance on corporate restructuring and pre-IPO preparation to ensure a successful listing

Investor Engagement & Positioning

- Support management teams in preparing and delivering investor presentations and roadshows

- Assist in positioning the company's equity story for institutional investors, HNIs, and anchor investors

- Build and leverage relationships across the investor ecosystem to drive subscription and post-listing performance

Candidate Profile

- Deep domain expertise in Indian capital markets with direct IPO transaction execution experience

- Proven track record of managing or advising on multiple SME IPO and/or Mainboard IPO transactions

- Strong understanding of SEBI ICDR Regulations and the end-to-end IPO documentation process

- Experience interacting directly with SEBI, NSE, BSE, and other regulatory authorities

- Strong network across capital market intermediaries, institutional investors, and legal advisors

- Strategic thinker who can operate at both a hands-on execution level and a senior advisory capacity

- Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills to interface with promoters, investors, and regulators

- Comfortable working in a flexible engagement model - full-time, advisory, or transaction-based

Technical Skills (Mandatory)

- In-depth working knowledge of SEBI ICDR Regulations, LODR Regulations, and Companies Act provisions relevant to IPOs

- Hands-on experience with DRHP/RHP drafting, review, and filing processes with SEBI and Stock Exchanges

- Strong understanding of IPO valuation methodologies, pricing mechanisms, and book-building processes

- Proficiency in financial modelling, due diligence frameworks, and transaction documentation

- Familiarity with capital market intermediary coordination - Merchant Bankers, RTAs, Legal Counsels, and CA / CS / MBA (Finance) / CFA or equivalent professional qualification - Preferred

- 10-15 years of experience in Indian capital markets transactions with direct IPO execution involvement (Mandatory)

- Experience working with SEBI-registered Merchant Banking firms, Capital Markets Advisory firms, or Investment Banks (Mandatory)

- Professionals currently or previously associated with Merchant Banking Firms, Boutique Investment Banks, IPO Advisory Firms, or Capital Markets Consulting Firms are strongly encouraged to apply

- Currently working professionals, independent consultants, and freelance advisors are all welcome to apply

data.job_title: Chartered Accountant - Finance & Global Accounting Analyst (Pune)
data.job_description:
PayGlocal is a global payments company that fosters global commerce. Our suite of offerings facilitate cross-border payments and solve the challenges surrounding it through three main pillars - the business, the experience and the associated risk.

Founded by finance veterans and experts, our understanding of merchant business, international business, security and regulatory standards help build impactful payment solutions. We partner with large enterprises and export merchants for seamless customer payment journeys.

Be a part of lasting innovation - create new products, new processes and new standards. Join us to help us shape the future of payments.

Position Summary

As a Chartered Accountant – Finance & Global Accounting , you will play a critical role in strengthening the finance backbone of a fast-growing cross-border payments business. You will be responsible for managing multi-entity accounting, supporting cross-border transaction flows, ensuring regulatory compliance across jurisdictions (India, US, UAE), and enabling accurate financial reporting.

This role is ideal for a qualified CA (0–2 years’ experience) who is detail-oriented, analytically strong, and eager to gain exposure to global accounting practices, fintech operations, and scalable financial processes in a high-growth environment.

What will you work on?

- Multi-Entity Accounting: Assist in preparation and finalization of books of accounts across multiple entities and jurisdictions (India, US, UAE, etc.).
- Financial Closures: Support month-end and year-end closing activities, including reconciliations, journal entries, and financial statement preparation.
- Inter-Company Transactions: Manage inter-company accounting, reconciliations, and cross-border charge validations.
- Transfer Pricing Support: Assist in transfer pricing documentation and ensure compliance with inter-company pricing regulations.
- Regulatory & Audit Compliance: Support statutory audits, tax audits, RBI/FEMA/GST compliance, and financial regulatory requirements.
- Cross-Border & FX Accounting: Work on cross-border payment flows, foreign exchange accounting, and revenue reconciliations.
- MIS & Reporting: Prepare MIS reports, variance analysis, financial dashboards, and performance tracking reports for leadership.
- Treasury Coordination: Assist in fund flow tracking, banking coordination, and liquidity monitoring.
- Process Improvement: Contribute to finance automation initiatives, ERP implementation support, and SOP documentation.
- Stakeholder Coordination: Work closely with auditors, consultants, tax advisors, and internal business teams.

Qualifications & Experience

- Qualification: Chartered Accountant (CA) – Qualified.
- Experience: 0–2 years of relevant experience (Articleship experience preferred).
- Accounting Knowledge: Strong understanding of accounting standards, financial statements, and reconciliation processes.
- Analytical Skills: Solid analytical and problem-solving capabilities with attention to detail.
- Technical Skills: Advanced proficiency in MS Excel; familiarity with ERP/accounting tools such as Zoho, Tally, NetSuite, QuickBooks, etc.
- Communication: Strong written and verbal communication skills with ability to coordinate across teams and external stakeholders.
- Work Environment Fit: Ability to thrive in a fast-paced fintech/startup environment with high ownership and accountability.

Additional Information:

• Exposure : Opportunity to work on global accounting practices across India, US, and UAE entities.

• Career Growth : High-ownership role with leadership exposure in a rapidly scaling fintech company.

• Learning & Development : Direct exposure to cross-border payments, regulatory frameworks, and scalable finance processes.

• Benefits : Health insurance and standard company benefits.

Target Profile:

• A recently qualified CA looking to build expertise in global and cross-border accounting.

• Strong interest in fintech, payments, and international finance.

• Detail-oriented professional with strong reconciliation and compliance mindset.

• Comfortable handling multi-entity accounting structures and regulatory complexity.

Who we are?

Johnson Controls is the global leader for smart, healthy and sustainable buildings. At Johnson Controls, we’ve been making buildings smarter since 1885, and our capabilities, depth of innovation experience, and global reach have been growing ever since. Today, we offer the world’s largest portfolio of building products, technologies, software, and services; we put that portfolio to work to transform the environments where people live, work, learn and play.

This is where Johnson Controls comes in, helping drive the outcomes that matter most. Through a full range of systems and digital solutions, we make your buildings smarter. We committed to invest 75 percent of new product development R&D in climate-related innovation to develop sustainable products and services. We take sustainability seriously. Achieving net zero carbon emissions before 2040 is just one of our commitments to making the world a better place.

As FP&A manager, work closely with the NA divisional finance teams to drive results.

What’s in it For You:
• You will join a growth company offering a competitive salary and benefits.
• The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.
• The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best.
• Feel proud each day to work for a company that provides clean water, safe food, abundant energy and healthy environments.

What You Will Do:
• Support the team of sales or gross margin or SG&A analysts​ to ensure that all deliverables are completed and divisional analytics packs are prepared and have the right analytics that support the underlying movements.
• Own the delivery of services as described in the service-level agreement with the divisions.
• Drive Divisional FP&A transformation goals to optimize, simplify, and automate P&A tasks.
• Partner with division finance teams to understand external and internal business dynamics and their impact on results​.
• Lead the development of the annual plan by working across multiple key stakeholders.
• Coordinate close and forecast activities, including communicating guidance, facilitating system loads, analyzing results, and preparing content for business reviews with senior leadership.
• Develop sustainable content for use in monthly business reviews (MBRs) and provide ad-hoc financial analytics and deep dives to facilitate decision making.
• Partners with broader finance and business stakeholders drive critical reporting needs and capabilities​.

Qualifications:
• Bachelor’s degree in finance, Accounting, or Economics
• CA or other professional preferred
• Minimum 12 years of Finance experience
• Relevant FP&A work experience ideally in an FMCG environment
• Proficiency in BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar platforms.
• Knowledge of financial and commercial reporting basics
• Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills.
• Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to interact effectively with stakeholders at all levels.
• Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment.

A trusted partner for millions of customers, Ecolab is a global sustainability leader offering water, hygiene and infection prevention solutions and services that protect people and the resources vital to life. Building on a century of innovation, Ecolab has annual sales of $15 billion, employs more than 47,000 associates and operates in more than 170 countries around the world. The company delivers comprehensive science-based solutions, data-driven insights and world-class service to advance food safety, maintain clean and safe environments, and optimize water and energy use. Ecolab’s innovative solutions improve operational efficiencies and sustainability for customers in the food, healthcare, life sciences, hospitality and industrial markets. www.ecolab.com

Position Description
The CITICS CLSA Equity Derivatives team (EQD) offers its clients a wide range of products and services meeting different needs like risk management, investment return enhancement, financing, and cross-border market access. EQD’s products and services include Prime Services, Derivative Products, Quantitative Investment Strategies (QIS), and Market Making.
EQD Quants is a team of quantitative analysts who specialize in quantitative finance and technology and work closely with EQD traders, sales and structuring teams. EQD quants design and implement EQDs e-trading infrastructure, market making systems, and trading systems for OTC non-linear, QIS, OTC prime and delta one.
This role will be primarily focused on researching and designing market making and trading strategies for EQD in India and other Asia-Pacific markets. The hire will report locally to the head of EQD Quant India and work closely with (a) EQD quants in Hong Kong and London, (b) EQD traders and structurers in Hong Kong, Singapore, Tokyo, London and New York and (c) EQD developers in India and other markets. The position will report to the EQD Quant India head in Mumbai.
Key Areas of Responsibilities
The day-to-day responsibilities of the EQD Quant will include—
• Analysis of market microstructure data to identify actionable trading signals for equities and derivatives in India and other Asia-Pac markets

• Analysis of order book data to identify actionable trading signals for equities and derivatives in India and other Asia-Pac markets

• Research and development of trading models using real-time and historical trade and quote data for equities and derivatives in India and other Asia-Pac markets

• Back-testing of the above strategies

• Understanding and enhancing EQD’s trading strategies for India and other Asia-Pac markets

• Working with EQD developers and the firm’s technology teams to implement trading strategies and optimizing code

• Understanding and contributing to EQD’s trading infrastructure

• Monitoring live performance of EQD’s trading strategies for continuous enhancement of signal detection and performance robustness

Requirements
• A bachelor’s degree in engineering from a top-tier Indian institute (e.g., the top IITs) and/or a master’s or PhD degree in a quantitative field (Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, Quantitative Finance, etc.) from a top-tier Indian or foreign university

• 6+ years of professional industry experience, ideally in research or development of trading strategies

• Deep knowledge of options, futures and other equity derivatives, especially in India

• Deep knowledge of market microstructure and exchange protocols in India/Asia-Pac markets

• Advanced knowledge of low-latency systems and real-time data processing

• Experience in trading or microstructure-based signal research using high-frequency or ultra-high frequency data in India/Asia-Pac markets

• Strong programming skills and experience in C++ (17.0 or later), Python or similar languages

• Self-starter with a passion and strong analytical and problem-solving skills

• Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and deliver solutions under pressure

• Excellent collaboration and communication skills

• Experience of trading on the NSE or BSE using low-latency or ultra-low-latency real-time data

• Experience of working on FPGAs

• Ability to adapt to new technologies and frameworks

About Company: The company operates in the financial technology industry, providing digital solutions that simplify and streamline international payment processes for businesses and professionals.

Job Description:

1. Monthly Close & Accounting Governance:

• Own and drive the monthly close calendar for India and global entities
• Review outsourced accounting entries for accuracy, completeness, and cut-off
• Ensure adherence to close timelines; escalate delays or gaps proactively
• Prepare quarterly consolidated financial statements
• Manage Accrual vs Cash P&L
• Draft and review accounting memos for non-routine / judgmental items
• Ensure adherence to transfer pricing policies
• Manage multi-entity readiness including:
• Intercompany accounting & reconciliations
• Cost allocations and cross-charges
• Consolidation preparedness (data, mappings, eliminations)
2. Reconciliations & Transaction Controls:
• Setting up the reconciliations process and own the outcome
• Track and resolve reconciling items within defined SLAs
3. Statutory, Tax & Regulatory Compliances:
• Own end-to-end compliances across India and global entities
• Ensure adherence to Tax laws, Companies Act, and payments regulations (PA-CB)
• Maintain and track a centralized compliance calendar across jurisdictions
• Ensure timely filings with zero compliance lapses or penalties
4. Audit Readiness & External Audit Management:
• Maintain continuous audit readiness (not just year-end)
• Act as the primary SPOC for auditors and drive timely closure of audit queries
5. Internal Controls & Risk Management:
• Strengthen and enforce internal control frameworks
• Support IFC/RCM documentation and testing readiness
• Drive process improvements across payments, vendors, revenue, BRS, etc.
6. Outsourced Accounting & Vendor Management:
• Coordinate with external accounting firms across geographies
• Enforce SLAs on timeliness, quality, and documentation hygiene
• Drive standardization and continuous improvement in outputs
7. Process Building & Automation:
• Design and implement scalable finance processes and internal control frameworks to support multi-entity, cross-border operations and rapid business growth
• Drive automation across accounting and finance workflows (month-end close, reconciliations, reporting, AP/AR) to improve accuracy, reduce manual dependency, and shorten closing timelines
• Evaluate, implement, and optimize finance systems and tools to enable real-time reporting, process standardization, and audit-ready documentation
8. Financial Reporting & MIS:
• Prepare monthly MIS, variance analysis, and management reporting packs
• Support budgeting and forecasting processes with data accuracy and insights
• Analyze revenue, costs, and unit economics to highlight financial risks and opportunities
9. Cash Flow & Treasury Support:
• Assist in cash flow forecasting and working capital management
• Monitor liquidity across entities and optimize fund movements
• Support banking relationships and treasury documentation

Qualifications & Experience:

• Qualified Chartered Accountant (CA)
• 4–6 years of post-qualification experience in controllership, accounting, or audit
• Fintech/payments industry experience preferred
• Strong ownership of month-end close, reconciliations, and audit readiness
• Experience working with outsourced accounting teams is a strong plus
• Comfortable with Zoho Books/Xero/ERP systems
• Experience in fast-growing startup environments is a plus

Ideal Candidate Profile

• Prefers review, reconciliation, validation, and sign-off over data entry
• Operates with strong close discipline: checklists, timelines, and exception tracking
• High-integrity operator who can push back on weak documentation or approvals
• Process-oriented with strong control and audit mindset
• Comfortable with fintech/payment complexities (settlements, multi-bank flows, regulatory expectations)
• Capable of managing a team and ensuring timely, accurate finance outcome

As a Senior Finance Director in our organization based in Pune, you will play a crucial role in leading and managing our Finance & Accounting team. With your 9-10 years of experience in finance, you will be responsible for various aspects such as statutory compliances, tax management, audits, and corporate finance.

- Oversee and manage tax and compliance litigation, ensuring adherence to TDS, GST, PF, PT, Shop Establishment, and other regulations.
- Ensure timely and accurate annual ROC filings and manage various audits including tax audits, statutory audits, internal audits, and group audits.
- Drive uniformity in client and vendor onboarding processes, implement segregation of duties, and ensure compliance and reconciliation.
- Manage month-end financial processes like reconciliation, accruals, and adjustments, as well as monitor cash flow and working capital efficiently.
- Lead and mentor a team of accountants to provide accurate and timely financial analysis.
- Build and maintain strong relationships with bankers, auditors, and other stakeholders.
- Identify and implement process improvements to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of the Finance & Accounting function.

- Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, or a related field. CA qualification is highly preferred.
- Minimum of 6 years of experience in finance, with expertise in tax compliance and auditing.
- In-depth knowledge of financial regulations and compliance requirements.
- Proficiency in using financial software and tools.
